name: SAAG ALOO CURRY
description: This is definitely one of our healthier curries, but it's also one of our most favourite! There's something irresistibly exciting about deep layers of flavour, the fenugreek being the star. We've made our spinach three ways to get maximum flavour out of this nutrient-packed super green. Creaminess and freshness. Serve this with a baked naan for a fully Indian sensation.
ingredients: Medium saucepan of boiling salted water on a high heat | Large frying pan on a medium heat | Liquidiser | Deep frying pan with a lid
3 tsp salt, plus a little extra
method: Peel the potatoes and cut them all in half. Put them into the medium saucepan and add just enough water to cover. Put the pan on a high heat and bring to the boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low and simmer until cooked, about 15-18 minutes. Take them off the heat and leave them in the hot water until needed.

Meanwhile, peel and finely chop the onion. Peel and mince the garlic. Add the oil to the large frying pan and add the cumin seeds and mustard seeds. Heat until they crackle and the cumin seeds start until they release their aroma, about 1 minute.

Add the chopped onion and garlic and stir until the onions are soft, about 10 minutes. Add the potatoes, the turmeric, garam masala and salt. Stir until the potatoes have a nice yellow coating from the turmeric.

Peel and finely dice the onion for the curry. Rip the stem from the big chilli, cut it in half lengthways, remove the seeds for a milder flavour (or leave them in for a curry with a kick) and finely chop. Peel and mince the garlic. Peel the ginger by scraping off the skin with a teaspoon and mince it. Finely chop the coriander.
Roughly chop 100g of the spinach and finely chop another 100g. Put the remaining 200g into the liquidiser. Add the coconut milk, tamarind and water and blend until smooth.

Add the oil to the deep frying pan. Scrape in the chopped onion, garlic and ginger and soften, about 10-15 minutes. Add the chilli and cook for 1-2 more minutes. Add the tomatoes and stir until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the garam masala and turmeric, the ground coriander, fenugreek, salt and sugar and stir until well combined.

Add the roughly chopped spinach and stir until completely wilted. Add the finely chopped spinach and stir to mix. Pour in the blended spinach mixture and stir to until you have a dark green sauce. Simmer until thickened, about 10 minutes.

Put in the cooked potatoes and tip in the potatoes. Stir to cover the potatoes and the sauce bubbling slightly. Squeeze over the juice of ½ the lemon, catching any pips in your other hand. Serve hot alongside rice or naan.
